title: Unknown(String) pattern: lossless round-trip is the correct default for forward-compatible enums
description: The Unknown(String) variant pattern (preserving the original wire value) is strictly better than unit Unknown + #[serde(other)] for any type used over a protocol wire. Unit Unknown loses the original string; re-serializing it produces the literal 'unknown', which is wrong if the server later sends a new known variant and the client echoes it back. Storing the string is the correct forward-compatible default and costs exactly one heap allocation per unknown value encountered. A future contributor might suggest reverting to #[serde(other)] for 'simplicity' — resist: the round-trip fidelity is a protocol correctness guarantee, not a style choice.
